sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for 1983 PC version

special_mechanics.name: Hole Digging
special_mechanics.description: Player can dig holes into floors to temporarily trap guards. Holes fill in after a short delay. Player cannot climb out of holes and will be killed if hole fills before escape. Guards drop gold when falling into holes.

physics_values.digging_constraints: only adjacent sides, not directly beneath
physics_values.multi_layer_digging: requires gap width equal to number of layers to dig through

special_mechanics.name: Guard Interaction Physics
special_mechanics.description: Player can safely walk over guards trapped in holes via feet-to-head contact. Player falls faster than guards. Contact during free fall is possible. Downward movement initiated on guard's head is fatal.

special_mechanics.name: Fall Damage
special_mechanics.description: Player character can fall from arbitrary heights without injury.

special_mechanics.name: No Jump Mechanic
special_mechanics.description: Player cannot jump. Movement is restricted to walking, climbing ladders, and using hand-to-hand bars.

tier_notes: Tier 2 - Empirical/Qualitative data only. Wikipedia source provides gameplay mechanics and physics interactions but no quantitative movement parameters (speeds, accelerations, gravity values). All documented values relate to mechanic descriptions rather than numerical physics values. Player movement characteristics inferred from gameplay descriptions (runs faster than guards, falls faster than guards) but exact values not provided.
sources_used: wikipedia - Lode Runner article (Gameplay, Enemy AI, Permitted contact, Trapping and using guards sections)
